reception: Considered one of the best films of the 2010s
legacy: Selected for preservation in the United States National Film Registry by the Library of Congress in 2025
structuredFacts.title: Inception
structuredFacts.subtitle: 2010 film by Christopher Nolan
structuredFacts.leadParagraph:
Inception is a 2010  science fiction action film written and directed by Christopher Nolan, who also produced it with Emma Thomas, his wife. The film stars Leonardo DiCaprio as a professional thief who steals information by infiltrating the subconscious of his targets. He is offered a chance to have his criminal history erased as payment for the implantation of another person's idea into a target's subconscious. The ensemble cast includes Ken Watanabe, Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Marion Cotillard, Elliot Page, Tom Hardy, Cillian Murphy, Tom Berenger, Dileep Rao, and Michael Caine.
After the completion of Insomnia in 2002, Nolan presented to Warner Bros. a written 80-page treatment for a horror film envisioning "dream stealers," based on lucid dreaming. Deciding he needed more experience before tackling a production of this magnitude and complexity, Nolan shelved the project and instead worked on Batman Begins (2005), The Prestige (2006), and The Dark Knight (2008). The treatment was revised over six months and was purchased by Warner Bros. in February 2009. Inception was filmed in six countries, beginning in Tokyo on June 19 and ending in Canada on November 22. Its official budget was $160 million, split between Warner Bros. and Legendary. Nolan's reputation and success with The Dark Knight helped secure the film's US$100 million in advertising expenditure.
Inception's premiere was held in London on July 8, 2010; it was released in both conventional and IMAX theaters beginning on July 16, 2010. Inception grossed $839 million worldwide, becoming the fourth-highest-grossing film of 2010. Considered one of the best films of the 2010s, Inception, among its numerous accolades, won four Oscars (Best Cinematography, Best Sound Editing, Best Sound Mixing, Best Visual Effects) and was nominated for four more (Best Picture, Best Original Screenplay, Best Art Direction, Best Original Score) at the 83rd Academy Awards. The Library of Congress selected Inception for preservation in the United States National Film Registry's class of 2025 for being "culturally, historically or aesthetically significant".
structuredFacts.briefSummary: Written and directed by Christopher Nolan, the 2010 science fiction action film Inception stars Leonardo DiCaprio as Dom Cobb, a professional thief who steals information directly from the subconscious. The plot intensifies when Saito hires Cobb and his partner Arthur to perform inception on Robert Fischer, aiming to dissolve his father's corporate empire. To synchronize their awakening across multiple dream levels, the team relies on Édith Piaf's French song 'Non, je ne regrette rien' as a vital audio cue. Following its theatrical release on July 16, 2010, the film grossed $839 million worldwide, culminating in its 2025 selection by the Library of Congress for permanent preservation in the United States National Film Registry.

cards.title: Inside the Multi-Layered Subconscious
cards.summary: To perform inception on corporate heir Robert Fischer, Saito hires extractors Dom Cobb and Arthur. Planting an idea directly into Fischer's subconscious mind requires high-stakes corporate espionage of the highest order. The team coordinates their escape using Édith Piaf's song 'Non, je ne regrette rien' as a vital signal. This auditory cue synchronizes their awakening across multiple collapsing dream levels. Meanwhile, Cobb battles the hostile projection of his deceased wife, Mal. Ultimately, navigating these shared dreams demands flawless timing to survive deep psychological dangers.
cards.readerQuestion: How do Dom Cobb and his team navigate the dangerous, shifting rules of shared dreaming?
cards.keyTakeaway: Shared dreaming requires precise synchronization amid deep psychological dangers.

cards.title: Architecting the Gravity Defying Heist
cards.summary: In 2002, Christopher Nolan pitched Inception to Warner Bros. as a horror film about dream stealers . Nolan sought to expand this eighty-page treatment into a massive, multi-layered sci-fi heist action film . Actor Joseph Gordon-Levitt performed all but one of his own stunts during the gravity-defying hallway fight . This transition resulted in a highly complex global production featuring groundbreaking physical stunt work and custom-tailored roles . Nolan wrote the character of Saito specifically for Ken Watanabe to collaborate again after Batman Begins . Ultimately, Nolan evolved a horror pitch into a physical action masterpiece .
cards.readerQuestion: How did Christopher Nolan transition his initial horror concept into a complex, physical production?
cards.keyTakeaway: Nolan evolved a horror pitch into a physical action masterpiece.

cards.title: Casting the Dream
cards.summary: Brad Pitt and Will Smith were the initial choices for the lead role of Dom Cobb . Christopher Nolan needed to build his blockbuster directing credentials and resolve major casting conflicts before bringing his complex dream heist concept to life . James Franco was originally in talks to play Arthur but had to pull out due to scheduling conflicts . The strategic delays and casting adjustments resulted in a highly refined script and a critically acclaimed final ensemble cast . Christopher Nolan shelved the project for several years to gain experience directing massive, complex productions . Strategic delays and casting shifts created the perfect cinematic team .
cards.readerQuestion: What major casting changes and strategic delays shaped the final cinematic team?
cards.keyTakeaway: Strategic delays and casting shifts created the perfect cinematic team.

cards.title: The Architecture of Dreams
cards.summary: Director Christopher Nolan released his original science fiction action film, Inception, on July 16, 2010. Audiences flocked to theaters, drawn by a highly complex heist thriller starring Leonardo DiCaprio. DiCaprio portrayed Dom Cobb, a skilled thief who extracts valuable secrets from deep within the subconscious. This mind-bending premise fueled immense critical acclaim and earned the production four Academy Awards. Ultimately, the film grossed $839 million worldwide to become the fourth-highest-grossing release of the year. Its triumph proved that challenging, original sci-fi can achieve massive global box office success.
cards.readerQuestion: How did a complex original sci-fi film capture the global box office in the summer of 2010?
cards.keyTakeaway: Original sci-fi achieved massive global box office success.

cards.title: Preserving a Modern Masterpiece
cards.summary: At the 83rd Academy Awards, Inception triumphed with four wins, including Best Cinematography and Best Visual Effects. Its groundbreaking technical execution and massive global box office success quickly established the film as a defining cultural phenomenon. Alongside these wins, it earned four additional nominations, competing for both Best Picture and Best Original Screenplay. These combined achievements paved the way for its eventual induction into the National Film Registry, cementing its status as a modern masterpiece. In 2025, the Library of Congress officially selected the film for permanent preservation within this prestigious archive. Ultimately, this blend of technical mastery and cultural impact secured its enduring place in cinematic history.
cards.readerQuestion: How did the film's technical achievements and cultural impact secure its place in film history?
cards.keyTakeaway: Technical mastery and cultural impact secured its permanent cinematic legacy.
